result:
The file is written to `tests/pub_figures.do`. Here's a walkthrough of what each section does:

**Figure 1 — Scatter with fit lines**
- Overlays four `twoway` layers: two scatter plots (one per `foreign` group) and two `lfit` lines
- Uses `%40` transparency on markers (navy for domestic, cranberry for foreign) to handle overplotting
- Distinct marker shapes (circle vs triangle) for B&W readability
- Legend placed inside the plot at 5-o'clock (`ring(0) position(5)`)

**Figure 2 — Bar chart with CI error bars**
- Uses `preserve`/`restore` to safely `collapse` without losing the original data
- Computes 95% CIs manually via `invttail(n-1, 0.025) * sd / sqrt(n)` (t-distribution, exact for each group's df)
- `twoway bar` + `rcap` (capped range spikes) renders the error bars — this is the standard workaround since `graph bar` doesn't support error bars natively

**Figure 3 — Combined + export**
- `graph combine` places both side-by-side (`rows(1)`)
- `iscale(0.85)` keeps text readable; `xsize(10) ysize(5)` gives a 2:1 aspect ratio
- Exports to PDF (vector, best for journals/LaTeX) and PNG at 2400px width

Run it in Stata with:
```stata
do tests/pub_figures.do
```

The exports (`combined_figure.pdf` and `combined_figure.png`) will be saved to Stata's current working directory.

tool_input.content:
// pub_figures.do — Three publication-quality figures from sysuse auto
// Requires: sysuse auto dataset (ships with Stata)

clear all
set scheme s1color
sysuse auto, clear

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Figure 1: Scatter of mpg vs weight, colored by foreign, with fit lines
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

twoway (scatter mpg weight if foreign == 0, ///
            mcolor(navy%40) msymbol(circle) msize(medsmall)) ///
       (scatter mpg weight if foreign == 1, ///
            mcolor(cranberry%40) msymbol(triangle) msize(medsmall)) ///
       (lfit mpg weight if foreign == 0, ///
            lcolor(navy) lwidth(medthick) lpattern(solid)) ///
       (lfit mpg weight if foreign == 1, ///
            lcolor(cranberry) lwidth(medthick) lpattern(solid)), ///
    title("Fuel Efficiency vs. Vehicle Weight") ///
    xtitle("Weight (lbs)") ytitle("Mileage (mpg)") ///
    xlabel(, format(%9.0fc)) ///
    legend(order(1 "Domestic" 2 "Foreign") ///
        position(5) ring(0) rows(2) ///
        region(lstyle(none))) ///
    graphregion(color(white)) plotregion(color(white)) ///
    name(scatter, replace) nodraw

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Figure 2: Bar chart of mean price by rep78 with 95% CI error bars
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

// Compute means and CIs (preserve original data)
preserve
drop if missing(rep78)
collapse (mean) mean_price = price ///
         (sd) sd_price = price ///
         (count) n = price, by(rep78)

generate ci_hi = mean_price + invttail(n - 1, 0.025) * sd_price / sqrt(n)
generate ci_lo = mean_price - invttail(n - 1, 0.025) * sd_price / sqrt(n)

twoway (bar mean_price rep78, ///
            barwidth(0.7) fcolor(navy%70) lcolor(navy)) ///
       (rcap ci_hi ci_lo rep78, ///
            lcolor(black) lwidth(medthin)), ///
    title("Mean Vehicle Price by Repair Record") ///
    xtitle("Repair Record (1978)") ytitle("Price (USD)") ///
    xlabel(1(1)5, valuelabel) ///
    ylabel(0(2000)12000, format(%9.0fc) angle(0)) ///
    legend(order(1 "Mean price" 2 "95% CI") ///
        position(2) ring(0) rows(2) ///
        region(lstyle(none))) ///
    graphregion(color(white)) plotregion(color(white)) ///
    name(barchart, replace) nodraw
restore

// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// Figure 3: Combined figure, exported as PDF and PNG
// ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

graph combine scatter barchart, ///
    rows(1) iscale(0.85) imargin(medium) ///
    title("Auto Dataset: Efficiency and Pricing", size(medium)) ///
    graphregion(color(white)) ///
    xsize(10) ysize(5)

graph export "combined_figure.pdf", replace as(pdf)
graph export "combined_figure.png", replace as(png) width(2400)
